title: openbindings.operation-graph execution fixture file
description: Describes one or more execution fixtures for the openbindings.operation-graph format. Each fixture pairs a graph, a set of mocked operation responses, and a graph input with the expected output stream, so a runner can replay the graph deterministically and compare. This schema constrains fixture shape only; a runner validates each `graph` against the op-graph schema separately.

section.description: The spec section or example this fixture is drawn from.

operations.description: Mocked behavior for each operation an `operation` node may invoke, keyed by operation key.

responses.description: Response rules, matched in order. The first rule whose `whenInput` deep-equals the node's input event (or whose `whenInput` is absent, a wildcard) applies.

whenInput.description: Input event this rule matches. Absent means it matches any input.

emit.description: Events the operation emits for a matched input. More than one element models a streaming operation.

fail.description: If present, the operation fails with this error code instead of emitting.

graph.description: An operation graph definition. A runner validates this against the op-graph JSON Schema.

input.description: The event provided to the graph's input node.

output.description: Expected operation-graph output events (events reaching the output node, plus any error:false exit event).

ordering.description: `exact` requires the output array to match in order; `set` requires the same multiset of events, since concurrency makes the order implementation-defined.

error.description: True when the graph is expected to terminate with an error (an exit node with error:true).

errorDetail.description: When error is true, the expected error-detail event.
